nodes.label: supabase-database Capability
nodes.rationale: New capability covering environment configuration, schema deployment, session middleware, and consistent Supabase client usage across the app.

nodes.label: Cookie-Aware Server Client
nodes.rationale: Server API routes must forward the user's session cookie for RLS. The singleton browser client in lib/supabase.ts does not carry session context on the server.

nodes.label: Auth Session Middleware Refresh
nodes.rationale: Root middleware.ts calls updateSession() on every matched request to refresh expired Supabase auth tokens via supabase.auth.getUser().

nodes.label: RLS Authenticated Access
nodes.rationale: Row-level security is enabled on campaigns, call_logs, contacts, security_logs, system_settings, and profiles; authenticated sessions are required for protected table access.

nodes.label: Legacy Browser Client Singleton
nodes.rationale: lib/supabase.ts browser singleton is retained for lib/simulator.ts and DEMO_MODE; API routes migrate to utils/supabase/server.

nodes.label: sip_trunks Table
nodes.rationale: Outbound trunk resolution joins campaigns.sip_trunk_id to sip_trunks.livekit_trunk_id for LiveKit SIP dial.

nodes.label: Outbound Calling Flow
nodes.rationale: Dispatch agent to room, create SIP participant, normalize phone, resolve trunk from campaign or env, record room name avm_{campaignId}_{contactId}_{rand}.

nodes.label: voice-recordings Storage Bucket
nodes.rationale: Private Supabase storage bucket for campaign voice_path objects; signed URLs via service-role admin client.

nodes.label: AVM Script Audio Storage
nodes.rationale: Campaign script MP3 uploads to Supabase-compatible S3 endpoint derived from NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL.

nodes.label: Inworld Voice Catalog
nodes.rationale: Curated Inworld TTS voice IDs with gender/ethnicity taxonomy for campaign agent voice selection.

nodes.label: Telephony Mock Phase 2 Migration
nodes.rationale: Mock localStorage telephony store for issue #27; method names mirror future API endpoints so Phase 2 replaces bodies with fetch to real LiveKit/SIP backend.

nodes.label: EVRA Chart Visual System
nodes.rationale: Centralised palette mapping call outcomes (connected, voicemail, qualified, hangup, etc.) to Chart.js colors and glow effects.

nodes.label: Dashboard Insight Registry
nodes.rationale: Declarative INSIGHTS registry rendering campaign/call metrics widgets; consumed by useDashboardLayout for pin/hide/order.

nodes.label: chart-visual-system capability
nodes.rationale: Dashboard charts used flat muted colours with indistinguishable green series; a centralised neon-inspired palette improves readability and brand cohesion without sacrificing operational comfort.

nodes.label: lib/chartTheme.ts central chart styling
nodes.rationale: Chart-specific semantics (outcome colours, glow, gradients) stay separate from general UI tokens in tokens.ts; extending tokens.ts only was rejected because the palette is large and helpers do not belong in general design tokens.

nodes.label: createBarGradient canvas helper
nodes.rationale: Chart.js requires canvas context at render time; a helper keeps Charts.tsx clean while enabling vertical bar gradients in Campaign Comparison.

nodes.label: Chart.js dataset shadowBlur glow
nodes.rationale: Built-in shadow properties apply glow to data elements only with minimal complexity; donut uses brighter stroke borders instead of per-slice glow.

nodes.label: Connected vs Qualified colour distinction
nodes.rationale: Connected (#47D16A green) and Qualified (#5BE8BE mint/aqua) must be visually distinct; per-agent colour tinting was removed for clarity.

nodes.label: Chart styling must not alter data logic
nodes.norm_label: chart styling must not alter data logic

nodes.label: evra-design-tokens capability
nodes.rationale: Blue-tinted glassmorphism (slate, Inter, #3b82f6) did not match EVRA brand; dark luxury-tech dashboard with green/mint accent and flat panels aligns with EVRA identity.

nodes.label: evra-mui-theme capability
nodes.rationale: MUI createTheme maps EVRA tokens to palette, typography, shape, and per-component overrides so most components inherit correct styling automatically.

nodes.label: evra-component-reskin capability
nodes.rationale: 15+ UI files need component-level updates to consume tokens and remove legacy glass/blue styling across layout shell, dashboard, auth, and modals.

nodes.label: Dual token layer CSS variables plus TypeScript
nodes.rationale: CSS variables enable global consistency; TypeScript exports are required where JS needs color strings (Chart.js, conditional sx). MUI-only or Tailwind @theme were rejected.
nodes.norm_label: dual token layer css variables plus typescript

nodes.label: EVRA dark as default and primary mode
nodes.rationale: EVRA spec is dark-only; maintaining two full palettes doubles migration effort with no design brief for light mode.
nodes.norm_label: evra dark as default and primary mode

nodes.label: GlassCard restyled to SurfaceCard
nodes.rationale: Glass naming is misleading after flat EVRA migration; rename clarifies intent with minimal import churn across 6-8 files.

nodes.label: Michroma display font via next/font
nodes.rationale: Only Michroma is required externally; body uses system stack and monospace for metrics to minimize font payload while matching EVRA spec.

nodes.label: Bottom-up theme migration order
nodes.rationale: Tokens first, then MUI theme, layout/fonts, primitives, shell, views, charts, QA — ensures each layer builds on established tokens.

nodes.label: EVRA color tokens as CSS custom properties
nodes.norm_label: evra color tokens as css custom properties

nodes.label: Flat EVRA Paper and Card surfaces
nodes.norm_label: flat evra paper and card surfaces

nodes.label: KPI and metric values use tabular monospace
nodes.norm_label: kpi and metric values use tabular monospace

nodes.label: legacy-outbound-calling capability
nodes.rationale: Team pivots away from Fonoster Routr as SIP routing layer; dead code and operational burden remain if Routr is kept while pursuing different telephony direction.

nodes.label: voip-provider-config capability
nodes.rationale: Carrier records stay in Supabase for operator configuration without external Routr sync, supporting future telephony direction at low cost.

nodes.label: Leaf-to-root Routr removal order
nodes.rationale: Delete in dependency order (tests, API routes, lib/routr, infrastructure, frontend, outbound-call, package config, DB migration, docs) to keep app buildable at each step. Feature-flag off was rejected — adds complexity with no planned re-enable.

nodes.label: Retain voip_providers SIP fields drop Routr sync columns
nodes.rationale: SIP credential fields remain useful for next telephony architecture; deleting entire carrier UI was rejected as low-value removal of useful config surface.
nodes.norm_label: retain voip_providers sip fields drop routr sync columns

nodes.label: Drop campaigns.routing_mode column entirely
nodes.rationale: Migration sets routr campaigns to legacy then drops column; keeping column with only legacy allowed was rejected as dead schema.

nodes.label: No Routr runtime in application stack
nodes.norm_label: no routr runtime in application stack

nodes.label: inworld-tts-api capability
nodes.rationale: Operators must record or upload voice files today; in-app Inworld TTS removes external recording step and enables script iteration before dialing.

nodes.label: campaign-voice-generation capability
nodes.rationale: New Campaign wizard Step 3 gets cascading voice picker, sample preview, script TTS, listen-back, and save-to-storage alongside existing upload.

nodes.label: Server-side TTS proxy not browser-direct
nodes.rationale: Inworld credentials must not reach the browser; POST /api/tts/generate matches existing API route trust boundary. Client-side public key was rejected.

nodes.label: Two-phase generate then save
nodes.rationale: Generate returns base64 for local preview; Save uploads to Supabase — avoids orphan storage objects and supports script iteration. Auto-upload on generate was rejected.

nodes.label: Voice catalog from voicelist.md suffix parsing
nodes.rationale: docs/voicelist.md is single source of truth; lib/inworld-voices.ts parses {gender}_{name}_{ethnicity} suffixes including _soft variants. Hardcoded JSON only was rejected as duplicate maintenance.
nodes.norm_label: voice catalog from voicelist.md suffix parsing

nodes.label: Reuse existing voice-recordings storage pipeline
nodes.rationale: Client-side upload via createClient().storage reuses proven CampaignModal path and RLS; dial-time resolveVoiceUrl signing flow unchanged. Server-side service-role upload rejected as unnecessary.

nodes.label: Upload vs Generate voice source toggle
nodes.norm_label: upload vs generate voice source toggle

nodes.label: TTS scope limited to CampaignModal v1
nodes.norm_label: tts scope limited to campaignmodal v1

nodes.label: Chart palette migration plan
nodes.rationale: Add chartTheme.ts, update tokens.ts aliases, refactor Charts.tsx, add CSS variables, manual QA. Rollback reverts four files with no data or API changes.

nodes.label: EVRA theme atomic migration plan
nodes.rationale: Ship all token and theme changes in single PR for visual consistency; no database or API migration; rollback is full PR revert with post-deploy smoke tests.

nodes.label: Routr removal deploy migration plan
nodes.rationale: Pre-deploy: apply DB migration, deploy without Routr services, test legacy outbound. Post-deploy: remove Routr container and routr-pgdata volume. Rollback only if Routr direction resumes.

nodes.label: Inworld TTS zero-DB migration plan
nodes.rationale: Add INWORLD_API_KEY, deploy API and UI (inactive if env missing), operator adds sample MP3s incrementally. No database migration. Rollback removes toggle and API route; upload flow unchanged.

nodes.label: Script Owns SIP Dial
nodes.rationale: dial-outbound.ts calls createDispatch and createSipParticipant; agent receives metadata only, matching LiveKit make_call recipe

nodes.label: Service Role Supabase Client
nodes.rationale: Script runs outside Next.js auth; SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY bypasses RLS for call_records insert and contacts update

nodes.label: Dial Script Dispatch and SIP
nodes.source_location: Requirement: Dial script dispatches agent then places SIP call

nodes.label: Web vs Routr Infrastructure Split
nodes.rationale: Next.js behind Cloudflare Tunnel cannot expose SIP ports; Routr requires dedicated VPS with public IP

nodes.label: Manual Routr Configuration (M1)
nodes.rationale: M1 configures Peer and Trunk via CLI/YAML on staging VPS before building Settings sync UX

nodes.label: Supabase Source of Truth for Routr
nodes.rationale: Admin edits voip_providers; API syncs to Routr on save; Routr has no operator UI
